IRS990.ActivityOrMissionDesc: STOWERS RESOURCE MANAGEMENT ("SRM") SUPPORTS THE ACTIVITIES OF STOWERS INSTITUTE FOR MEDICAL RESEARCH ("SIMR") WHICH CONDUCTS BASIC CELL AND MOLECULAR BIOLOGY RESEARCH.
IRS990.Desc: FINANCIAL SUPPORT - SRM MONITORS THE FINANCIAL NEEDS OF SIMR SO SIMR CAN CONDUCT ITS MEDICAL RESEARCH IN THE PUBLIC INTEREST AND MAKES CONTRIBUTIONS TO SIMR TO SUPPORT ITS MEDICAL RESEARCH ACTIVITIES. THE CONTRIBUTIONS ARE MADE AS DETERMINED BY SRM'S BOARD OF DIRECTORS AFTER REVIEW OF FINANCIAL INFORMATION, BUDGETS, STRATEGIES AND OTHER RELEVANT INFORMATION. IN 2021, SRM PROVIDED $120,000,000 TO SIMR FOR ENDOWMENT SUPPORT AND $21,000,000 TO BIOMED VALLEY CORPORATION ("BVC"), A RELATED 501(C)(3) ORGANIZATION, FOR ENDOWMENT SUPPORT.

ProgSrvcAccomActy2Grp.Desc: MANAGEMENT, ADMINISTRATIVE AND OPERATION SUPPORT SERVICES - SRM PROVIDES MANAGEMENT SUPPORT TO SIMR THAT INVOLVES ASSISTING IN PLANNING FOR THE STRATEGIC DIRECTION OF SIMR,OPERATIONAL PLANNING, AND THE DEVELOPMENT AND IMPLEMENTATION OF STRATEGIC BUSINESS PLANS AND BUDGETS. SRM ALSO PROVIDES ADDITIONAL ADMINISTRATIVE AND OPERATIONAL SUPPORT SERVICES TO SIMR THAT INCLUDES ACCOUNTING, FINANCE, HUMAN RESOURCES, INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY, AND OTHER SUPPORT ACTIVITIES, FOR WHICH SRM ALLOCATED COSTS TO SIMR IN THE AMOUNT OF $31,014,701 IN 2021 AS REPORTED ON PART VIII STATEMENT OF REVENUE LINE 2A, WITH RELATED INFORMATION ON SCHEDULE O.

ProgSrvcAccomActy3Grp.Desc: MANAGEMENT OF ENDOWMENT - SRM PARTICIPATES IN THE MANAGEMENT OF STOWERS INSTITUTE FOR MEDICAL RESEARCH ("SIMR") TO INSURE SIMR'S FINANCIAL ABILITY TO CONTINUE ITS RESEARCH EFFORTS IN PERPETUITY.

Form990PartVIISectionAGrp.TitleTxt: DIR OF EHS QTY & REG SYS

ProgramServiceRevenueGrp.Desc: REIMB. FROM BVD, RELATED C CORP

Form990ScheduleAPartVIGrp.ExplanationTxt: SRM PROVIDES A WIDE-RANGE OF SERVICES AND SUPPORT TO SIMR AS EXPLAINED IN PART III PROGRAM SERVICES, LINES 4B AND 4C.

SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE PERMANENT RESTRICTED ENDOWMENT, INVESTED IN PERPETUITY, PROVIDES A SOURCE OF INCOME TO FUND THE "PRISCILLA WOOD NEAVES ENDOWED CHAIR IN BIOMEDICAL SCIENCE" AT THE STOWERS INSTITUTE. THE EARNINGS FROM THE ENDOWMENT WILL BE DISTRIBUTED, ACCORDING TO THE TERMS OF THE RESTRICTED GIFT, TO THE CURRENT HOLDER OF THE CHAIR TO BE USED IN THEIR PURSUIT OF INNOVATIVE RESEARCH. THE TEMPORARILY RESTRICTED ENDOWMENT(S) PROVIDE FUNDING FOR CERTAIN SCIENCE AND OTHER ACTIVITIES AT THE STOWERS INSTITUTE ACCORDING TO THE TERMS OF THE GIFT(S).

FIN 48 FINANCIAL STATEMENT FOOTNOTE FROM THE CONSOLIDATED STATEMENTS OF STOWERS INSTITUTE FOR MEDICAL RESEARCH (THE INSTITUTE), OF WHICH STOWERS RESOURCE MANAGEMENT, INC. IS A SUPPORTING ORGANIZATION, IS AS FOLLOWS: THE INSTITUTE RECOGNIZED THE EFFECT OF INCOME TAX POSITION ONLY IF THOSE POSITIONS ARE MORE LIKELY THAN NOT OF BEING SUSTAINED. RECOGNIZED INCOME TAX POSITIONS ARE MEASURED AT THE LARGEST AMOUNT THAT IS GREATER THAN 50% LIKELY OF BEING REALIZED.

SRM'S PURPOSE IS TO SUPPORT THE ACTIVITIES OF SIMR, WHICH CONDUCTS BASIC CELL AND MOLECULAR BIOLOGY RESEARCH. BESIDES GIVING FINANCIAL SUPPORT TO SIMR, SRM ALSO HELPS MANAGE SIMR'S ENDOWMENT, PROVIDES MANAGEMENT SUPPORT AND FUNDRAISES FOR ITS BENEFIT. THE MANAGEMENT SUPPORT INVOLVES ASSISTING IN PLANNING FOR THE STRATEGIC DIRECTION OF SIMR, OPERATIONAL PLANNING, DEVELOPMENT AND IMPLEMENTATION OF STRATEGIC BUSINESS PLANS, INCLUDING FINANCE, LEGAL, HUMAN RESOURCES AND OTHER CORPORATE FUNCTIONS. THE CONTRIBUTIONS, GRANTS AND INCOME FROM SRM'S ENDOWMENT ARE USED TO SUPPORT THE EXEMPT ACTIVITIES OF SIMR.
FINANCIAL SUPPORT TO ASSIST WITH SUPPORT ORGANIZATION'S SCIENTIFIC DISCOVERIES AND GIFT ANNUITY OBLIGATION.
FINANCIAL SUPPORT TO ASSIST WITH BVC'S EXEMPT PURPOSE TO SUPPORT, BENEFIT, AND CARRY OUT THE PURPOSES OF ITS SUPPORTED ORGANIZATION.

RltdOrgOfficerTrstKeyEmplGrp.TitleTxt: DIR OF EHS QTY & REG SYS

SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: SRM HAS A WRITTEN EMPLOYMENT CONTRACT WITH RICHARD W. BROWN (PRESIDENT/CEO), WHICH PROVISIONS INCLUDE PAYMENT OF SOCIAL CLUB DUES AND PERSONAL USE OF AN AUTOMOBILE. SRM HAS A WRITTEN EMPLOYMENT CONTRACT WITH RODERICK L. STURGEON (EVP/CFO), WHICH PROVISIONS INCLUDE REIMBURSEMENT FOR PERSONAL USE OF AN AUTOMOBILE, TERM LIFE INSURANCE, TAX PLANNING AND TAX RETURN FEES, AND FINANCIAL PLANNING SERVICES. SRM HAS A WRITTEN EMPLOYMENT AGREEMENT WITH CHARLES GERMAN (EVP/GENERAL COUNSEL), WHICH PROVISIONS INCLUDE REIMBURSEMENT FOR PROFESSIONAL AND SOCIAL CLUB DUES. ALL TAXABLE BENEFITS ARE TREATED AS COMPENSATION TO THE RECIPIENTS. STURGEON'S EMPLOYMENT AGREEMENT ALSO PROVIDES FOR HIM TO RECEIVE TAX GROSS-UP PAYMENTS SUFFICIENT TO COVER THE TAXES ON THE TAXABLE BENEFITS.

SRM PROVIDED THE BENEFITS DESCRIBED IN RESPONSE TO LINE 1A PURSUANT TO WRITTEN EMPLOYMENT CONTRACTS THAT WERE APPROVED BY SRM'S GOVERNING BOARD. SEE RESPONSE TO FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 15 FOR ADDITIONAL PROCESSES RELATED TO DETERMINING SALARY AND BENEFITS FOR OFFICERS.

IssuerName.BusinessNameLine1Txt: HEALTH & ED FACILITIES AUTHORITY OF MO
HEALTH & ED FACILITIES AUTHORITY OF MO
HEALTH & ED FACILITIES AUTHORITY OF MO
HEALTH & ED FACILITIES AUTHORITY OF MO

SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: $290,000,000 BOND ISSUE IN AGGREGATE CONSISTING OF $75,000,000 OF SERIES 2015A BONDS, $75,000,000 OF SERIES 2015B BONDS, $75,000,000 OF SERIES 2015C BONDS, AND $65,000,000 OF SERIES 2015D BONDS.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: PART I, COLUMN F FOR LINES A, B, C, AND D:
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: SRM AND STOWERS INSTITUTE FOR MEDICAL RESEARCH ("SIMR") ARE THE TWO MEMBERS OF AN OBLIGATED BOND GROUP FOR THESE TAX-EXEMPT BONDS; SRM IS A SUPPORTING ORGANIZATION OF SIMR AS DESCRIBED IN SRM'S MISSION STATEMENT AND REPORTED ON SCHEDULE R. THE BONDS WERE ISSUED TO REFUND $215,000,000 OF BONDS ISSUED IN 2000 AND $75,000,000 OF BONDS ISSUED IN 2002. THE 2000 BOND SERIES WAS ORIGINALLY ISSUED FOR THE PURPOSE OF PURCHASING AN EXISTING MEDICAL CENTER AND SURROUNDING BUILDINGS, STRIPPING THE STRUCTURES TO THEIR INFRASTRUCTURE AND RECONSTRUCTING A STATE OF THE ART MEDICAL RESEARCH FACILITY FOR SIMR. THE 2002 BOND SERIES WAS ORIGINALLY ISSUED FOR THE PURPOSE OF INTERIOR FINISH, FURNISHING, AND EQUIPPING OF THE SHELL FOR THE STRUCTURES BUILT WITH THE EARLIER 2000 BONDS.
AGGREGRATE 2015ABCD BOND ISSUE OF $290,000,000 WAS ISSUED TO REFUND $215,000,000 BOND SERIES ISSUED IN 2000 AND $75,000,000 BOND SERIES ISSUED IN 2002.
AGGREGRATE 2015ABCD BOND ISSUE OF $290,000,000 WAS ISSUED TO REFUND $215,000,000 BOND SERIES ISSUED IN 2000 AND $75,000,000 BOND SERIES ISSUED IN 2002.

THE BOND PROCEEDS WERE USED TO REFUND AND REDEEM THE REFUNDED BONDS ON THE ISSUE DATE. IN ADDITION, THE INSTITUTE REASONABLY EXPECTS THAT THE DEBT SERVICE FUND WILL QUALIFY AS A BONA FIDE DEBT SERVICE FUND IN EVERY BOND YEAR. BASED ON THESE EXPECTATIONS AND CERTIFICATIONS, BOND COUNSEL HAS ADVISED THE AUTHORITY AND THE INSTITUTE THAT NO ARBITRAGE REBATE OR YIELD RESTRICTIONS WILL BE REQUIRED FOR THE BONDS.
THE AMOUNT SHOWN IN PART I, COLUMN (B) FOR "NUMBER OF CONTRIBUTIONS" REPRESENTS THE TOTAL NUMBER OF CONTRIBUTIONS.

THE STOWERS RESOURCE MANAGEMENT INC. ("SRM") OPERATES EXCLUSIVELY WITHIN THE MEANING OF CODE SECTIONS 501(C)(3) AND 509(A)(3). SRM'S CHARITABLE PURPOSE IS TO SUPPORT, BENEFIT, AND CARRY OUT THE PURPOSES OF ITS SUPPORTED ORGANIZATION. SRM CURRENTLY SUPPORTS STOWERS INSTITUTE FOR MEDICAL RESEARCH ("SIMR"). SIMR IS A TAX EXEMPT ORGANIZATION DESCRIBED IN SECTION 501(C)(3), AND PUBLIC CHARITY DESCRIBED IN SECTIONS 509(A)(1) AND 170(B)(1)(A)(III). SIMR IS ACTIVELY ENGAGED IN SCIENTIFIC RESEARCH IN THE PUBLIC INTEREST. SRM HELPS MANAGE SIMR'S ENDOWMENT, PROVIDES MANAGEMENT SUPPORT, AND FUNDRAISES FOR ITS BENEFIT. THE MANAGEMENT SUPPORT INVOLVES ASSISTING IN PLANNING FOR THE STRATEGIC DIRECTION OF SIMR, OPERATIONAL PLANNING, DEVELOPMENT, AND IMPLEMENTATION OF STRATEGIC BUSINESS PLANS AND BUDGETS. SRM ALSO PROVIDES ADDITIONAL ADMINISTRATIVE AND OPERATIONAL SUPPORT SERVICES TO SIMR THAT INCLUDES ACCOUNTING, FINANCE, HUMAN RESOURCES, INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY, AND OTHER CORPORATE SUPPORT FUNCTIONS. THE CONTRIBUTIONS, GRANTS, AND INCOME FROM SRM'S ENDOWMENT ARE USED TO SUPPORT THE EXEMPT ACTIVITIES OF SIMR.

THE BOARD HAS DELEGATED CERTAIN AUTHORITY TO AN EXECUTIVE COMMITTEE WHICH MAY EXERCISE DELEGATED POWERS AND AUTHORITY OF THE BOARD IN THE MANAGEMENT OF THE BUSINESS AND AFFAIRS OF SRM. THE MEMBERS OF THE EXECUTIVE COMMITTEE ALL SERVE ON THE BOARD.

RICHARD W. BROWN, DAVID A. WELTE, ALEJANDRO SANCHEZ ALVARADO, VIRGINIA G. STOWERS THROUGH 6/5/2021, JONATHAN THOMAS, GINA KAISER, ALBERZINE FREEMAN, CHARLES GERMAN AND BRENT KREIDER, ALL DIRECTORS OF SRM, HAVE A BUSINESS RELATIONSHIP. CLIFFORD W. ILLIG, JONATHAN THOMAS, ALBERZINE FREEMAN, BRENT KREIDER, RICHARD W. BROWN, RODERICK L. STURGEON AND CHARLES GERMAN, ALL DIRECTORS OF SRM, HAVE A BUSINESS RELATIONSHIP. BRENT KREIDER AND ALEJANDRO SANCHEZ ALVARADO, DIRECTORS OF SRM, HAVE A BUSINESS RELATIONSHIP. VIRGINIA G. STOWERS THROUGH 6/5/2021 AND JAMES E. STOWERS III, DIRECTORS OF SRM, HAVE A FAMILY RELATIONSHIP.
SIMR IS SRM'S SOLE MEMBER AND ELECTS SRM'S BOARD OF DIRECTORS.

THE DATA AND INFORMATION NECESSARY TO PREPARE SRM'S FORM 990 IS COMPILED BY THE ACCOUNTING DEPARTMENT AND REVIEWED BY OUR TAX ATTORNEY AT BRYAN CAVE LEIGHTON PAISNER, LLP. PWC, OUR EXTERNAL TAX PREPARERS, USES THIS INFORMATION TO PREPARE THE FORM 990. THE COMPLETED FORM 990, INCLUDING REQUIRED SCHEDULES, IS REVIEWED BY THE OFFICERS OF SRM BEFORE IT IS FILED WITH THE IRS. AFTER THE PREPARATION AND REVIEW PROCESS DETAILED ABOVE, THE FORM 990, INCLUDING REQUIRED SCHEDULES, IS PROVIDED TO EACH VOTING MEMBER OF SRM'S BOARD BEFORE IT IS FILED WITH THE IRS.

SRM HAS ADOPTED A "CONFLICTS OF INTEREST AND DIRECTOR INDEPENDENCE POLICY". EACH DIRECTOR, OFFICER, AND OTHER PERSON WHO IS IN A POSITION TO EXERCISE SUBSTANTIAL INFLUENCE OVER DECISIONS OF SRM ARE REQUIRED TO ANNUALLY COMPLETE AND SIGN A DISCLOSURE STATEMENT. A COVERED PERSON MUST DISCLOSE THE EXISTENCE OF A POTENTIAL CONFLICT AND ALL MATERIAL FACTS TO THE GOVERNING BOARD AS SOON AS THE PERSON HAS KNOWLEDGE THAT A POTENTIAL CONFLICT MIGHT EXIST. SRM ALSO CONDUCTS PERIODIC AND ADHOC REVIEWS OF TRANSACTIONS AND AGREEMENTS TO ENSURE THAT IT DOES NOT ENGAGE IN ACTIVITIES THAT ARE NOT CONSISTENT WITH ITS TAX-EXEMPT PURPOSE.

THE PROCESS FOR DETERMINING THE REASONABLENESS OF THE COMPENSATION FOR SRM'S PRESIDENT, BOTH EXECUTIVE VICE-PRESIDENT'S/GENERAL COUNSEL, AND VICE-PRESIDENT OF SCIENCE COMMUNICATION INCLUDED RETAINING THE SERVICES OF A NATIONALLY KNOWN COMPENSATION CONSULTANT TO PROVIDE DATA AND ANALYSIS TO DETERMINE COMPARABLE SALARY AND BENEFITS FOR SIMILARLY QUALIFIED PERSONS IN COMPARABLE POSITIONS AT SIMILARLY SITUATED ORGANIZATIONS. COMPENSATION FOR ALL POSITIONS WAS DOCUMENTED IN EMPLOYMENT CONTRACTS WHICH WERE APPROVED BY THE GOVERNING BODY AS DOCUMENTED IN THE OFFICIAL MINUTES. THE LAST COMPLETED REVIEW PROCESS FOR SRM'S PRESIDENT WAS IN 2019. THE EXECUTIVE VICE-PRESIDENT/GENERAL COUNSEL REVIEW WAS COMPLETED IN 2020.
STOWERS RESOURCE MANAGEMENT INC.'S GOVERNING DOCUMENTS, CONFLICT OF INTEREST POLICY, AND FINANCIAL STATEMENTS ARE AVAILABLE UPON REQUEST.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: PART VII, SECTION A, COLUMN B:
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: RICHARD W. BROWN, RODERICK L. STURGEON THROUGH 5/2/2021, PENNY SPENCE AS OF 5/3/2021 AND CHARLES GERMAN, OFFICERS OF SRM, ALSO PERFORM SUPPORT SERVICES FOR ONE OR MORE OF THE RELATED ORGANIZATIONS DISCLOSED IN SCHEDULE R. THESE SERVICES ARE PERFORMED IN THEIR ROLE AS SRM EMPLOYEES AND SRM IS REIMBURSED BY THE RELATED ORGANZIATIONS.

SRM RECEIVES INVESTMENT MANAGEMENT SERVICES FROM AMERICAN CENTURY INVESTMENTS ("ACI"). ACI IS A WHOLLY OWNED SUBSIDIARY OF AMERICAN CENTURY COMPANIES, INC. ("ACCI"). IN SELECTING ACI TO MANAGE ITS LIQUUID INVESTMENTS, SRM NOT ONLY SELECTED A HIGH QUALITY MUTUAL FUND COMPANY WITH AN OUTSTANDING TRACK RECORD, BUT ALSO PLACED ITS LIQUID INVESTMENTS IN A COMPANY IN WHICH IT OWNS STOCK AND RECEIVES DIVIDENDS. SRM PAYS ACI THE SAME ADMINISTRATIVE FEES FOR THESE SERVICES AS ANY ARMS-LENGTH INVESTORS.

SRM RECEIVED EXPENSE REIMBURSEMENTS FROM RELATED PARTIES, AS DESCRIBED IN SCHEDULE R, FOR ADMINISTRATIVE SUPPORT SERVICES BY SRM. SRM ALLOCATED THE COSTS OF PROVIDING THESE SERVICES TO THE RELATED PARTIES.

OTHER CHANGES IN NET ASSETS OR FUND BALANCES EQUITY TRANSFERS FROM RELATED 501(C)(2) ORG (NOTE 1) $2,154,551 UNREALIZED GAIN ON INTEREST SWAP 2,144,976 ANNUITY PAYMENT TO SIMR 15,000,000 ACTUARIAL CHANGE IN ANNUITY OBLIGATION (NOTE 2) 65,888,105 ---------------- OTHER CHANGES IN NET ASSETS $85,187,632. NOTE 1: SRM RECEIVED PASS-THROUGH AMOUNTS FROM STOWERS REAL ESTATE HOLDING CORPORATION ("SREHC"). SRM IS THE TAX-EXEMPT PARENT OF SREHC. SREHC IS A TAX-EXEMPT ORGANIZATION UNDER SECTION 501(C) (2) OF THE CODE, AND AS SUCH, SREHC REMITS THEIR NET INCOME AND MAKES OTHER TRANSFERS TO ITS TAX-EXEMPT PARENT, SRM. SRM RECEIVED $2,154,551 OF NET INCOME FROM SREHC. NOTE 2: IN MARCH 2001, THE INSTITUTE RECEIVED AN UNRESTRICTED CONTRIBUTION OF SHARES OF ACCI CLASS A NON-TRADED COMMON STOCK WITH AN APPRAISED VALUE OF $1,160,409,000 FROM THE INSTITUTE'S CO-FOUNDERS, JIM AND VIRGINIA STOWERS, IN EXCHANGE FOR AN ANNUITY PAYABLE TO THE DONORS WITH THE PAYMENTS CONTINUING THROUGHOUT THE LIFE OF THE LAST SURVIVING DONOR. IN MARCH 2014, JIM STOWERS PASSED AWAY LEAVING VIRGINIA STOWERS AS A SOLE RECIPIENT OF THE ANNUAL ANNUITY PAYMENT. IN JUNE 2021, VIRGINIA STOWERS PASSED AWAY. AS SHE WAS THE SOLE RECIPIENT OF THE ANNUAL ANNUITY PAYMENT, HER PASSING EXTINGUISHED THE ANNUITY LIABILITY.

STOWERS POLICY INSTITUE INC'S ("SPI") PRIMARY ACTIVITY IS TO PROMOTE INNOVATIVE AND ETHICAL BIOMEDICAL RESEARCH, THERAPIES AND CURES, AND TO ADVOCATE FOR A POLITICAL, ECONOMIC AND SOCIAL ENVIRONMENT THAT ADVANCES, FACILITATES, AND PROTECTS THAT RESEARCH AND THOSE THERAPIES AND CURES.
BIOMED VALLEY DICOVERYIES, INC'S ("BVD") PRIMARY ACTIVITY IS THE DEVELOPMENT AND MARKETING OF SCIENTIFIC DISCOVERIES. BIOMED VALLEY CORPORATION ("BVC") IS THE 100% OWNER OF BVD STOCK.
SRM PROVIDES RESOURCES AND SERVICES TO ITS RELATED ORGANIZATIONS DESCRIBED IN PART II AND PART III AND IS REIMBURSED BY THE RELATED ORGANIZATIONS.

STOWERS REAL ESTATE HOLDING CORPORATION ("SREHC") IS A CONTROLLED ENTITY WITHIN THE MEANING OF 512(B)(13). HOWEVER, SREHC MADE NO SPECIFIED PAYMENTS TO SRM. RATHER, SRM RECEIVED PASS-THROUGH AMOUNTS FROM SREHC. SREHC IS A TAX EXEMPT ORGANIZATION UNDER 501(C)(2) OF THE CODE, AND AS SUCH, REMITS ITS NET INCOME AND MAKES OTHER TRANSFERS TO ITS TAX EXEMPT PARENT, SRM. SRM RECEIVED $2,154,551 OF NET INCOME FROM SREHC IN 2021.
